Canada.name: Canada
Canada.rank: #30 (per FIFA's latest rankings ahead of the tournament) FOX Sports
Canada.rivals: a generally friendly rivalry with the United States, which plays out most often in the Gold Cup, with the two now considered CONCACAF's two premier teams; the history with Mexico is far more lopsided — Canada has lost 22 of 35 all-time meetings since 1957, though recent results have been closer. Wikipedia + 2

timeline.event: World Cup debut in Mexico; lose all three games without scoring. World Cup Ranking
Win the CONCACAF Gold Cup, beating Colombia 2-0 in the final — still their only continental title. FIFA World Cup News
End the 36-year World Cup drought; Davies scores their first-ever World Cup goal against Croatia, but they exit after three defeats. World Cup RankingWikipedia
Automatically qualify for the 2026 World Cup as co-hosts. Wikipedia
Open the home tournament with a 1-1 draw against Bosnia and Herzegovina. Wikipedia

players.name: Alphonso Davies
players.club: Bayern Munich (captain, expected to miss the first two group games with a hamstring injury) FourFourTwo

players.name: Jonathan David
players.club: Juventus (Canada's all-time leading scorer, enters as one of their biggest attacking threats) Yahoo Sports

players.name: Stephen Eustáquio
players.club: FC Porto (vice-captain, the screening midfielder shielding the back line) Squawka

Colombia.name: Colombia
Colombia.rivals: Argentina is the defining rival, from the 1994 qualifying shock to a heartbreaking 1-0 loss in the 2024 Copa América final. Grokipedia

timeline.event: Colombian Football Federation founded.
First World Cup appearance.
Stun Argentina 5-0 in Buenos Aires during qualifying, fueling talk of Colombia as a genuine title contender. Al Día News
Group-stage exit overshadowed by Escobar's own goal and his subsequent murder — a tragedy that ended the golden generation. Al Día News
Best-ever finish, reaching the quarter-finals as James Rodríguez wins the Golden Boot. MLSsoccer.com
Clinch 2026 qualification with a 3-0 win over Bolivia. mexc

players.name: James Rodríguez
players.club: Minnesota United (captain, second all-time leading scorer for the national team) MLSsoccer.com

players.name: Luis Díaz
players.club: Bayern Munich (the new face of the attack) Decatur Daily

players.name: David Ospina
players.club: Atlético Nacional (Colombia's most-capped player) Wikipedia

DR Congo.name: DR Congo
DR Congo.rivals: Senegal, after the dramatic 2-0-to-3-2 collapse that cost them automatic qualification, and Nigeria, their play-off final opponents, with Nigeria later filing a complaint to FIFA over the Leopards' use of allegedly ineligible players. ESPN

timeline.event: First international, played as Belgian Congo against Northern Rhodesia. Wikipedia
Become the third African nation to play at a World Cup, as Zaire. Sport News Africa
Win the Africa Cup of Nations twice. Wikipedia
Lead Senegal 2-0 in Kinshasa before conceding three late goals to lose 3-2, missing direct qualification. Sport News Africa
Beat Cameroon in the African play-off semi-final, then Nigeria in the final. aol
Beat Jamaica 1-0 after extra time in Guadalajara to seal a first World Cup return in 52 years. Dawan Africa

players.name: Chancel Mbemba
players.club: Lille (captain, the nation's most-capped player with 107 appearances) Wikipedia

players.name: Yoane Wissa
players.club: Newcastle United (the Leopards' dangerman) Olympics

players.name: Cédric Bakambu

C\u00f4te d'Ivoire.name: Côte d'Ivoire
C\u00f4te d'Ivoire.rivals: Ghana is the principal rival, including AFCON finals in both 1992 and 2015, alongside Nigeria (the 2024 AFCON final) and old continental foe Cameroon. Australia Football

timeline.event: First international.
First AFCON title, winning an 11-10 penalty shootout over Ghana. Football History
Three consecutive World Cups with the Drogba-Touré generation, never past the group stage. NewsX
Second AFCON title, again on penalties (9-8) against Ghana. Football History
Win a stunning third AFCON title at home after nearly being eliminated in the group stage and changing coaches mid-tournament. Australia Football
Return to the World Cup, opening with a 1-0 win over Ecuador. FotMob

players.name: Franck Kessié
players.club: Al-Ahli (captain, reached 100 international caps earlier in 2026) World Cup Wiki

Croatia.name: Croatia
Croatia.rivals: Serbia is the defining rivalry, rooted in the breakup of Yugoslavia and the wars of the 1990s, regarded as one of the most hostile in world football. Wikipedia

timeline.event: Admitted to FIFA ranked 125th. Wikipedia
World Cup debut ends in third place, beating the Netherlands 2-1. gulfnews
Reach their first World Cup final, losing to France.
Third place again, beating Morocco in the play-off. ESPN
Modrić aims to become only the third male player ever to appear at six World Cups. World Cup Pass

players.name: Luka Modrić
players.club: AC Milan (captain, chasing his 200th cap) World Cup Pass

players.name: Josko Gvardiol
players.club: Manchester City (Croatia's premier defender, back after shin surgery) World Cup Pass

players.name: Mateo Kovačić
players.club: Manchester City (one of four Croatian centurions, links defense and attack) World Cup Pass

Cura\u00e7ao.name: Curaçao
Cura\u00e7ao.rivals: Jamaica, after the decisive qualification battle for first place in CONCACAF qualifying, and Trinidad & Tobago, one of the traditional Caribbean rivalries dating back to the former Netherlands Antilles era.

timeline.event: First international match played by the Netherlands Antilles, the predecessor team to modern Curaçao.
Following the dissolution of the Netherlands Antilles, Curaçao begins competing as a separate national team.
Admitted to FIFA, allowing the nation to enter World Cup qualifying independently.
Win their first Caribbean Cup, defeating Jamaica in the final.
Reach the CONCACAF Gold Cup quarter-finals for the first time, narrowly losing to the United States.
A 0-0 draw away to Jamaica secures first-ever World Cup qualification.

players.name: Leandro Bacuna
players.club: captain and the experienced heartbeat of the side.

players.name: Eloy Room
players.club: veteran goalkeeper and one of the architects of the qualification campaign.

players.name: Livano Comenencia
players.club: highly rated young midfielder who scored Curaçao's first-ever World Cup goal.

Czechia.name: Czechia
Czechia.rank: around #39 in the June 2026 FIFA rankings.
Czechia.rivals: Slovakia, stemming from the peaceful split of Czechoslovakia in 1993, while historic encounters with Germany and Poland remain among the most significant fixtures in Central European football.

timeline.event: First international played by the Kingdom of Bohemia, a precursor to the modern national team.
Czechoslovakia reach their first World Cup final, losing 2-1 to Italy in Rome.
Reach a second World Cup final before falling to Brazil.
Win the European Championship, remembered for Antonín Panenka's iconic penalty in the final.
First World Cup appearance as an independent nation.
Return to the World Cup after a 20-year absence by winning the UEFA play-offs.

players.name: Tomáš Souček
players.club: West Ham United captain and the team's aerial threat from midfield.

players.name: Patrik Schick
players.club: Bayer Leverkusen striker and Czechia's leading goalscoring weapon.

players.name: Vladimír Coufal
players.club: experienced defender and key leader in the squad.

Ecuador.name: Ecuador
Ecuador.rivals: Peru is the traditional rivalry due to decades of border disputes and closely contested World Cup qualifying campaigns, while Colombia has become another frequent competitive opponent in modern CONMEBOL football.

timeline.event: Join FIFA.
Qualify for a first-ever World Cup under Hernán Darío Gómez.
Reach the Round of 16 in Germany, their best World Cup performance to date.
Return to the World Cup with one of the tournament's youngest squads.
A 0-0 draw away to Peru confirms qualification for the 2026 World Cup.
Enter the tournament having qualified for five of the last seven World Cups.

players.name: Moisés Caicedo
players.club: Chelsea midfielder and the engine of Ecuador's team.

players.name: Willian Pacho
players.club: Paris Saint-Germain defender and one of South America's most highly regarded centre-backs.

players.name: Kendry Páez
players.club: teenage playmaker widely regarded as the country's next global star.
